title: Amy Schumer's Career Arc, and 'Snatched' Director Jonathan Levine
format: Episode 14: New York Times comedy critic Jason Zinoman on Amy Schumer's rise and the scrutiny that came with it, then 'Snatched' director Jonathan Levine on taking a for-hire gig, ripping off 'Boogie Nights', assisting Paul Schrader, and clearing his AFI debt.

segments.heading: Amy Schumer's Career Arc
segments.note: with Jason Zinoman, New York Times comedy critic

films.title: Trainwreck
films.note: Her first movie and a big hit; Zinoman notes it had a Judd Apatow claim on it that 'Snatched' does not.

films.title: Snatched
films.note: The sophomore vehicle, with Goldie Hawn 'selling tickets.' Zinoman calls it a truer test than 'Trainwreck': around $32M so far, and despite the bad reviews, a bankable step.

films.title: Snatched
films.note: His reactive next move after a personal film: a for-hire comedy he could rework on set with Schumer, Kim Caramele, and Katie Dippold. Did well financially, less so critically, 'a first' for him.

films.title: The Night Before
films.note: The Seth Rogen Christmas comedy he wrote and re-outlined two months out at Rogen and Evan Goldberg's insistence. He thinks it has a shot as a cable-discovered cult classic.

films.title: The Wackness
films.note: His personal, point-of-view-driven breakout; he wants to write a sequel and misses the stylistic agility it allowed.
His favorite of his own films, and one he 'had so little to do with the writing of.'

films.title: Warm Bodies
films.note: He was the only writer on it; last-minute voiceover rewrites 'made the movie take a quantum leap forward.'

films.title: All the Boys Love Mandy Lane
films.note: Paid about $15K for two and a half years; he sold the editing computer on Craigslist. Its sale was the first real money he saw.

films.title: 21 Jump Street
films.note: One end of his 'Snatched' reference stack: 'everything from 21 Jump Street to Fitzcarraldo.'

films.title: Fitzcarraldo
films.note: The other end of the same reference stack.

films.title: Bob & Carol & Ted & Alice
films.note: Watched to prep 'I'm Dying Up Here.'

films.title: Boogie Nights
films.note: Ripped off repeatedly: the yardstick he used to talk himself into risky shots.

films.title: The Killing of a Chinese Bookie
films.note: Cassavetes; the red-light club look was 'stolen directly from that.'

films.title: This Is the End
films.note: One of the Rogen/Goldberg movies their producers and financiers had already made, which made the mid-shoot rewriting safe.

films.title: Auto Focus
films.note: Schrader was editing it while Levine assisted him; 'a movie I really, really like.'

films.title: Taxi Driver
films.note: Levine found Schrader's original yellow-legal-pad outline in the office files: the whole thing planned before a word was written. His favorite movie.

films.title: A Bigger Splash
films.note: His favorite movie of the previous year: 'so beautiful, so cool, so weird,' and a high-level Ralph Fiennes performance.

Books: Letterman: The Last Giant of Late Night

All the Boys Love Mandy Lane.title: All the Boys Love Mandy Lane

The Killing of a Chinese Bookie.title: The Killing of a Chinese Bookie
